suggestions: Task 4 depends on Task 7 (PR generation) but the frontend's data-fetching hook (subtask 4001) has no dependencies and can't actually integrate without the PM server endpoints from Task 7 being available — make the dependency explicit at the subtask level or add a decision point about whether to mock the API contract or wait for Task 7 completion.

Task 3 (Hermes integration) should include a decision point about the Hermes API contract stability — whether to code against a versioned schema, what happens if the response shape changes, and whether a client SDK or raw fetch is appropriate, since the entire integration is built on an assumed response shape ({ title, summary, url, relevance_score }).

Tasks 9 and 10 could enable more parallelism: Task 10 depends entirely on Task 9, but several subtasks (10001 ServiceAccounts, 10003 RBAC Roles, 10006 Audit Policy, 10007 NetworkPolicies) have no actual structural dependency on Task 9's HPA/Ingress/probe work — they could be split into a parallel track or Task 10's dependency on 9 could be narrowed to only the deployment subtasks that need the prod namespace and deployments to exist.

Fix the invalid dependency graph: remove Task 4’s dependency on Task 7 (frontend UI can ship against stubbed/mock PR endpoints), or split Task 7 into (7a) “persist PR metadata + expose /api/pipeline/:runId/pr(s) endpoints” (depends on 2, can unblock 4) and (7b) “actual GitHub PR creation + commit flow”; then make Task 4 depend on 7a instead of 7.

Reduce unnecessary serial chains by separating “cluster infra prerequisites” from “code changes”: allow Tasks 2/3/5/7 to start without Task 1 as a hard dependency by adding explicit config interfaces + local env fallback (e.g., accept env vars directly in dev), and keep Task 1 as a deployment/environments task needed only for in-cluster validation/E2E (Task 8).

Add explicit decision points (with options) where feasibility is currently hand-wavy: (a) NetworkPolicy egress strategy in dev/prod (DNS + FQDN policies vs. CIDRs vs. allow-all with audit) since “resolve IPs” is brittle, and (b) secret rotation source-of-truth (External Secrets + cloud store vs. manual rotation runbook) because the proposed CronJob cannot actually mint new Linear/GitHub/Discord credentials without an external system.

Defer tasks 9 and 10 entirely from this plan — production hardening (HA scaling, CDN, TLS, RBAC, secret rotation, audit logging) is v2 scope; the E2E pipeline can be validated without production infra, and these 19 subtasks represent ~40% of the plan's bulk with zero v1 value. Remove the dependency chain requiring E2E completion (task 8) before hardening can begin — this sequencing alone proves they're post-launch concerns.

Collapse subtask 2002 (caching) into 2001 (resolve_agent_delegates implementation) — a module-level cache variable is 3 lines of code, not a standalone subtask. Similarly collapse 2004 (remove legacy label) into 2003 (integrate delegates into issueCreate), and collapse 10008 (security.md) into task 10's acceptance criteria rather than a separate subtask. These inflations obscure real work.
